Thanks so much for your interest in this hackathon! Unfortunately, we've given out all available credits. You're still welcome to join using the free tier — we'd hate for that to stop you from participating. Sorry for the disappointment, and thanks for your understanding.
to connect with other participants, follow along in the #hackathon-announcements channel for news and #office-hours to attend a local office hours near you!
We’re collaborating with Codex Ambassadors to host more than 60 events around the world—
and hack alongside the community in person next week.
We’re also hosting virtual programming with members of the OpenAI product and developer teams, including office hours, livestreams, and OpenAI Academy sessions—
explore the full Build Week lineup
Start with the problem, not the model.
The strongest builds solve something real and use GPT-5.6 because the problem called for it — not the other way around.
in the OpenAI Discord before you're heads-down. The best teams form in week one, not the night before the deadline.
Record your demo as you go.
A clear 3-minute video beats a rushed one every time. Show it working and review the rules for what’s required: a voiceover explaining how you used Codex and GPT-5.6.
Judges may not build it themselves — make sure they
, with clean instructions and sample data.
Anything beyond your free/granted Codex credits is on you, so keep an eye on the meter.

Thanks so much for your interest in this hackathon! Unfortunately, we've given out all available credits. You're still welcome to join using the free tier — we'd hate for that to stop you from participating. Sorry for the disappointment, and thanks for your understanding.
to connect with other participants, follow along in the #hackathon-announcements channel for news and #office-hours to attend a local office hours near you!
We’re collaborating with Codex Ambassadors to host more than 60 events around the world—
and hack alongside the community in person next week.
We’re also hosting virtual programming with members of the OpenAI product and developer teams, including office hours, livestreams, and OpenAI Academy sessions—
explore the full Build Week lineup
Start with the problem, not the model.
The strongest builds solve something real and use GPT-5.6 because the problem called for it — not the other way around.
in the OpenAI Discord before you're heads-down. The best teams form in week one, not the night before the deadline.
Record your demo as you go.
A clear 3-minute video beats a rushed one every time. Show it working and review the rules for what’s required: a voiceover explaining how you used Codex and GPT-5.6.
Judges may not build it themselves — make sure they
, with clean instructions and sample data.
Anything beyond your free/granted Codex credits is on you, so keep an eye on the meter.

to
          find teammates, and start conversations by clicking the "Message" button.
P.S. Ensure your status is set to Looking for teammates.

OpenAI Build Week is a chance to explore what's possible with GPT-5.6 and Codex. Experiment with new ideas, connect with other builders, and submit a project for a chance to win prizes and recognition.

The Devpost Hackathons plugin runs inside ChatGPT and includes everything you need to participate—from challenge requirements and rules to the submission flow. It can help you brainstorm ideas, plan your project, and prepare your submission without leaving Codex.
Create a project using Codex with GPT 5.6. Whether it's an app, agent, website, game, workflow, or something entirely new, your project should fit into one of the following tracks:
Consumer apps for everyday life — from productivity and creativity to home, family, travel, health, and personal finance.
Tools that make teams faster or more effective, from workflow automation and customer support to analytics, sales, and back-office operations.
Tools for developers, including testing, DevOps, agentic workflows, and security.
Projects that specifically push forward AI for education - either helping students, teachers, or educational organizations.
